data.narrative:
Oxford Nanopore Technologies is a publicly traded biotech company founded by Hagan Bayley, Gordon Sanghera, and Spike Willcocks. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables in Biotech & Health, alongside 3 public-market reference tickers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

The peer-median method is the primary applicable triangulation approach. Four public biotech peers—Tempus AI ($6.0B), Freenome ($2.6B), Recursion Pharmaceuticals ($1.8B), and Alto Neuroscience ($529M)—yield a median valuation of $2.2B, with a range spanning $529M to $6.0B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples cannot be applied because target revenue is not available on file; the sector median P/S of 5.8x across the three public tickers remains inaccessible without disclosed revenue.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.9B to $2.5B, centered on $2.2B. This narrow band reflects reliance on a single methodological pillar. Key uncertainties include revenue visibility (absent from disclosed data), growth trajectory relative to peer benchmarks, and the wide dispersion within the peer set itself—a 11x spread from floor to ceiling suggests material heterogeneity in business stage or market expectations that a simple median may not capture. Validation against additional metrics (EBITDA multiples, R&D spend ratios) would strengthen conviction but remains unavailable from the present dataset.
